name: Járngreipr
description: Járngreipr, meaning 'iron grippers' or 'iron grabbers,' are a pair of iron gloves possessed by the mighty god Thor in Norse mythology. These gloves are not merely protective; they are essential for wielding his legendary hammer, Mjölnir. Mjölnir is so powerful that only Thor, with the aid of Járngreipr and Megingjörð (his belt of strength), can effectively wield it. Without the gloves, the hammer would burn and shatter anything Thor tried to hold, including his own hands. Járngreipr amplify Thor's already immense strength, allowing him to grip Mjölnir securely and direct its destructive force with precision. They symbolize Thor's control over his power and his preparedness to defend Asgard and Midgard from threats. The gloves are a vital part of Thor's arsenal, ensuring he can perform his duties as a protector of gods and humans. They represent the necessary tools and preparations even the strongest beings need to manage overwhelming power. Járngreipr are more than just gloves; they are a testament to the importance of control, preparation, and the understanding of one's own capabilities and limitations in the face of immense power. Their existence highlights the nuanced nature of strength and the need for tools to harness it effectively. They are a permanent fixture of Thor's image and a symbol of his preparedness.
subtitle: Iron gloves essential for wielding Mjölnir.
origin: Forged by the dwarven blacksmiths, likely the same craftsmen who created Mjölnir.

keyMyths.title: Thor's Power
keyMyths.description: Járngreipr are essential for Thor to wield Mjölnir. Without them, the hammer would burn his hands and be uncontrollable.

keyMyths.title: Protection from Mjölnir
keyMyths.description: The gloves protect Thor from the immense heat and power generated by Mjölnir when it is wielded.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, mentioning Thor and his equipment.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, mentioning Thor and his equipment.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems, including those featuring Thor and his hammer.
